meta.manuscript_location: Shrine of the Book, Israel Museum, Jerusalem

preamble.summary: Chapter 20 is a brief prose narrative (6 verses) describing Isaiah's symbolic act of walking naked and barefoot for three years as a sign against Egypt and Cush. The prose style yields very few variants.
preamble.notable_variants: No significant variants. The narrative is preserved nearly identically in both traditions.

variant_notes: No significant variant. The scroll reads identically to the MT here.
1QIsaiah-a writes השק without the sin/shin distinction visible in the MT's pointing. The consonantal text is identical. No impact on meaning.
No significant variant. The scroll reads identically to the MT here.
Identical consonantal text. The shame of Egyptian and Cushite captives is described identically. No impact on meaning.
